name: Daily Note
short_description: This is a single player, story driven psychological thriller that follows the mental collapse of a character who has lost touch with reality. As the player, you enter the mind of someone trying to escape a world that is falling apart. Remember the past by reading a diary left behind by your family.
detailed_description:
This game offers a single-player, story-driven psychological thriller experience that goes beyond an ordinary narrative, dragging you deep into the mind.
In this dark journey where the line between reality and fiction becomes increasingly blurred, players take control of a character who has lost touch with reality.

The protagonist lost his family during a camping trip—yet he remembers nothing about this traumatic event. Buried memories and repressed emotions begin to resurface through his daughter’s diary. As the pages turn, the player witnesses the character’s mental unraveling, forgotten memories, and suppressed fears.

Reality is fracturing. Silence speaks. Memory cannot be trusted.
At the core of the game lie the fragile nature of the human mind, personal introspection, and the process of coping with trauma. Moving through whispers, fragmented memories, and muffled recollections, the experience delivers a narrative that is both thought-provoking and deeply affecting.

Developed by a solo creator, the game stands out with its powerful visual language and atmosphere. Every location, use of light, and environmental detail has been meticulously crafted to reflect the protagonist’s mental state. Sound design and compelling voice acting further enhance the storytelling, forging a stronger emotional connection with the player.

If you're seeking an atmospheric, emotional, and thought-provoking experience, this game doesn't just tell you a story—it makes you a part of it.
This world will either consume you… or you will consume it.

Who will you believe? The family calling out to you from the fragments of your shattered memories, trying to wake you up… or the silence that whispers everything is just fine?

This world may not belong to you… but a part of you is still fighting. Even your mind is waging war on your behalf—so whose side are you on?

supported_languages:
English*, Simplified Chinese, Turkish
*languages with full audio support
